name: iso-27001
description: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A control rules covering asset management, risk assessment, access control, cryptography, operations security, communications security, and incident management. Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition.

severity_rules.pattern: New service or data store added to the project without a corresponding asset-inventory entry (owner, classification, criticality).
severity_rules.description: Annex A.5.9 requires an inventory of information and other associated assets with documented ownership.
severity_rules.remediation: Add the asset to the inventory with owner, classification (public/internal/confidential), and criticality tier.

severity_rules.pattern: High-risk feature (authentication, payment, PII processing, third-party integration) merged without a referenced risk-assessment artifact.
severity_rules.description: Clause 6.1.2 requires a documented information-security risk assessment for material changes.
severity_rules.remediation: Link the PR to the risk assessment doc; require security sign-off before merge for high-risk paths.

severity_rules.pattern: Code grants standing administrative access on production systems without a just-in-time elevation flow.
severity_rules.description: Annex A.8.3 requires access-rights management consistent with the principle of least privilege.
severity_rules.remediation: Replace standing admin grants with JIT elevation that records reason and expires automatically.

severity_rules.pattern: Code uses a deprecated or weak cipher (MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, ECB-mode) for data protection or message authentication.
severity_rules.description: Annex A.8.24 requires the use of cryptography aligned with industry-standard guidance.
severity_rules.remediation: Replace with SHA-256+, AES-GCM, Ed25519/ECDSA-P256, and Argon2/bcrypt for password hashing.

severity_rules.pattern: Production component has no centralised logging or monitoring hook for security-relevant events.
severity_rules.description: Annex A.8.15 requires event logging for security-relevant actions and continuous monitoring.
severity_rules.remediation: Ship logs to a central SIEM with retention; alert on authentication, authorisation, and integrity events.

severity_rules.pattern: Network egress from production to an external network without an explicit allow-list rule and traffic inspection.
severity_rules.description: Annex A.8.21 requires that information transferred via networks is protected via segregation and inspection.
severity_rules.remediation: Place egress behind an allow-list proxy with TLS termination and traffic inspection.

severity_rules.pattern: Project lacks an incident-response runbook or has no on-call rotation wired to security alerting.
severity_rules.description: Annex A.5.26 requires a documented incident-response process with assigned responsibilities.
severity_rules.remediation: Author a runbook covering detection, containment, eradication, recovery; wire alerts to an on-call rotation.

severity_rules.pattern: Code references a third-party SaaS or API without recording it in the supplier register with security review status.
severity_rules.description: Annex A.5.32 requires a supplier register documenting information-security obligations and review cadence.
severity_rules.remediation: Add the supplier to the register with contract reference, security review date, and renewal cadence.
